existing_idea: A SOC-2 audit prep tool for solo founders that auto-collects evidence from GitHub, Vercel, and Stripe, organizes it into the right AICPA controls, and generates a board-ready report — all without needing a compliance consultant.
insider_knowledge: Fraud analysts at every payments company I've worked at manually copy evidence into audit spreadsheets for 3 weeks every year. It's tedious and error-prone and nobody has automated it because auditors don't want to rock the boat.
network: About 15 payments and fraud professionals from past jobs who I can text for feedback, plus a small Slack group of fintech engineers (~50 people).
audience: Small Twitter following (~1200) in the fintech/fraud-detection niche; occasional blog posts on Substack (~300 subscribers).
proprietary_access: Access to anonymized fraud pattern datasets from prior employers (with permission), paid subscriptions to fraud industry research (Aite-Novarica, Forrester).
rare_combinations: Distributed systems engineering + first-hand fraud-analyst workflow knowledge + SOC-2 audit experience.
recurring_frustration: Internal fraud tooling at every payments company is 5 years behind what's technically possible, because the people with the money to buy aren't the people who use the tools.
four_week_mvp: A CLI that reads a GitHub repo and a Vercel project, matches files/settings against SOC-2 controls, and emits a PDF evidence bundle.
previous_attempts: Tried building a 'fraud tooling' company in 2022, got as far as 5 customer interviews, killed it because the sales cycle was too long for a solo founder.
customer_affinity: Solo technical founders building B2B SaaS in regulated industries — I am one and I understand their pain.
trigger: My current employer is requiring every engineer to go through a SOC-2 evidence collection sprint and I realized the tooling is a joke.
legal_constraints: Non-compete with current employer expires in 4 months, covers only direct-competitor products.
additional_context: I've read every paper on multi-agent compliance systems I could find. I'm obsessed with the idea of turning audit prep from a 3-week panic into a continuous background process. Inspired by 'Seeing Like a State' and the way bureaucracies legitimize themselves through paperwork. I want this to become a lifestyle business I run from a cabin in Vermont, not a VC rocketship.
